title: Getting Started with Spring Boot
content:
### Spring Initializr

Visit [start.spring.io](https://start.spring.io) to bootstrap a new Spring Boot project. Select the build tool (Maven or Gradle), language (Java, Kotlin, Groovy), Spring Boot version, and dependencies (starters).

### Project Structure

```
my-project/
├── pom.xml (or build.gradle)
├── src/
    ├── main/
    │   ├── java/
    │   │   └── com/example/
    │   │       └── MyApplication.java
    │   └── resources/
    │       ├── application.properties
    │       └── static/ (or templates/)
    └── test/
        └── java/
```

### @SpringBootApplication

This is a convenience annotation that combines `@Configuration`, `@EnableAutoConfiguration`, and `@ComponentScan`. The main class should be placed in the root package.

```java
@SpringBootApplication
public class MyApplication {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        SpringApplication.run(MyApplication.class, args);
    }
}
```

### Build and Run

Use the Maven wrapper (`mvnw`) or Gradle wrapper (`gradlew`):
```bash
./mvnw spring-boot:run
./mvnw package && java -jar target/my-app.jar
```

quiz.question: Which three annotations are combined by @SpringBootApplication?
Where should you place the main Spring Boot application class?
options: In the root package above all other packages
Anywhere, component scan finds it automatically
